instructions: Create a new file `src/jitsu/core/client.py` containing an `LLMClientFactory` class responsible for calling `dotenv.load_dotenv()`, verifying the `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, and returning an initialized `instructor` client. Refactor `JitsuPlanner` (in `planner.py`) and `JitsuExecutor` (in `executor.py`) to accept this client via constructor injection rather than duplicating the instantiation logic. Update `tests/core/test_planner.py` and `tests/core/test_executor.py` to inject a mocked client.
anti_patterns: Do NOT leave any `dotenv.load_dotenv()` or `instructor.from_openai()` calls inside the planner or executor classes.
Do NOT use global variables for the client; it must be passed via dependency injection.
completion_criteria: `JitsuPlanner` and `JitsuExecutor` receive their LLM client via Dependency Injection.
All core tests pass without requiring deep patching of `os.environ` or `dotenv`.

instructions: Create a `ProviderRegistry` dictionary in `src/jitsu/providers/registry.py` (or export it via `__init__.py`) that maps provider string names to their respective classes (e.g., `{"file": FileStateProvider, ...}`). Refactor `ContextCompiler.__init__` in `src/jitsu/core/compiler.py` and `_handle_request_context` in `src/jitsu/server/mcp_server.py` to import and use this centralized registry instead of defining their own hardcoded dictionaries.
anti_patterns: Do NOT manually construct dictionaries mapping strings to provider classes inside `compiler.py` or `mcp_server.py`.
Do NOT cause circular imports between the Core/Server layers and the Provider layer.
completion_criteria: A centralized `ProviderRegistry` acts as the single source of truth for available providers.
`ContextCompiler` and the MCP Server dynamically load from this registry.
The codebase successfully adheres to the Open/Closed Principle for adding new providers.

instructions: Create a `CommandRunner` utility in `src/jitsu/core/runner.py` (or `jitsu.utils`) to encapsulate all `subprocess.run` calls. Ensure it uses `shutil.which("just")` to dynamically resolve the `just` binary. Refactor `JitsuExecutor.execute_directive` and the MCP server's `_handle_git_commit` in `src/jitsu/server/mcp_server.py` (which currently hardcodes `/opt/homebrew/bin/just`) to utilize this new `CommandRunner`.
anti_patterns: Do NOT use hardcoded paths like `/opt/homebrew/bin/just` anywhere in the codebase.
Do NOT leave direct `subprocess.run` calls in the executor or server modules.
completion_criteria: `CommandRunner` successfully isolates all OS-level subprocess executions.
The MCP Server no longer crashes on non-MacOS environments due to hardcoded Homebrew paths.
Execution and server tests pass using the new runner.
